# AGENTS.md — carrier-gateway

## SDD Context

This project uses **Spec-Driven Development (SDD)**. All feature work follows the phase pipeline:

`init -> explore -> propose -> spec + design -> tasks -> apply -> review -> verify -> clean -> archive`

### openspec/ Directory

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `openspec/config.yaml` | Project configuration, tech stack, conventions |
| `openspec/specs/` | Merged specifications (source of truth for current system) |
| `openspec/changes/` | Active change proposals with phase artifacts |
| `openspec/changes/archive/` | Completed and archived changes |

### Build & Verify Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `go vet ./...` | Static analysis / type checking |
| `golangci-lint run ./...` | Linting |
| `go test ./...` | Test suite |
| `gofmt -l .` | Format checking |
| `go build ./...` | Build |

## Code Review Rules

### ALL FILES

REJECT if:

- Empty error checks (`if err != nil { }` with no handling)
- Unhandled returned errors (discarded without explicit `_ =` assignment)
- Use of `unsafe` package without documented justification
- Hardcoded secrets, tokens, API keys, or credentials in source
- `fmt.Println` / `log.Println` in production code (use structured logger)
- Magic numbers or magic strings without named constants
- Nesting depth exceeding 3 levels
- Files exceeding 800 lines
- Dynamic code execution with untrusted input (`os/exec` with unsanitized args)
- `interface{}` instead of `any` or typed interfaces (Go 1.18+)
- Panic in library code (panics allowed only in `main` or test code)

REQUIRE:

- All exported functions and types have doc comments
- Errors wrapped with context using `fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err)`
- Table-driven tests with `t.Run` subtests
- Explicit error returns at public API boundaries
- `context.Context` as first parameter in functions that do I/O or may block
- Graceful shutdown handling for long-running goroutines
- Channel and goroutine lifecycle management (no goroutine leaks)
- Input validation at API/handler boundaries

PREFER:

- Small, focused interfaces (1-3 methods) over large interfaces
- Constructor functions (`NewXxx`) over direct struct initialization
- Dependency injection over global state
- Early returns over deep nesting
- Named return values only when they improve readability
- `errors.Is` / `errors.As` over string comparison for error checking
- Immutable data patterns where practical
- Descriptive variable names (`duration` not `d`, `carrier` not `c`)

### GO CONCURRENCY

REJECT if:

- Goroutine launched without cancellation mechanism (context or done channel)
- Shared mutable state accessed without synchronization (mutex, atomic, or channel)
- Unbounded goroutine spawning (must use worker pool or semaphore pattern)

REQUIRE:

- `sync.WaitGroup` or `errgroup.Group` for fan-out/fan-in patterns
- Select statements include `ctx.Done()` or timeout case
- Channels are closed by the sender, never the receiver

PREFER:

- `errgroup.Group` over manual `sync.WaitGroup` + error collection
- Channel-based communication over shared memory with locks
- `sync.Once` for one-time initialization

### HEXAGONAL ARCHITECTURE

REJECT if:

- Domain layer imports infrastructure packages (adapter, handler, or external libs)
- Infrastructure types leak into domain interfaces
- Business logic placed in handler/adapter code

REQUIRE:

- Port interfaces defined in the domain layer
- Adapters implement domain-defined port interfaces
- Clear separation: domain/ (core), ports/ (interfaces), adapters/ (implementations)
